body:
## Summary
- Restores the static-site asset reference extractor for scalar HTML attributes, responsive `srcset`/`imagesrcset`, media `poster`, inline CSS URLs, and linked stylesheet URLs.
- Adds recursive local CSS `@import` traversal with a visited set/depth guard, resolving imported stylesheet URLs relative to each stylesheet.
- Adds temp-site fixtures proving imported stylesheet assets and responsive/poster references fail manifest membership when omitted.

## Validation
- PASS: `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node --check scripts/update-site-asset-manifest.js`
- PASS: `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node --check scripts/static-site-asset-references.test.js`
- PASS: `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node --test --test-name-pattern 'linked CSS imports|CSS import and url assets|responsive images|responsive and poster|referenced assets omitted' scripts/static-site-asset-references.test.js`
- PASS: `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false` with a temporary local `node_modules` symlink to the main checkout dependencies
- PASS: `git diff --check`

Known current-main blockers outside this claim:
- `npm run test:static-site-asset-references` still fails on the stale `signin.js` ban and stale committed `site/asset-manifest.json`.
- `node --test scripts/check-live-site.test.js --test-name-pattern "asset manifest"` still fails because `package.json` does not yet wire `node scripts/update-site-asset-manifest.js --check` into `test:static-site-asset-references`.
title: static-site: follow CSS imports in asset guard
